titleStmt.title: The Chargé in the United Kingdom ( (Atherton) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Chargé in the United Kingdom ( Atherton ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 373. As reported in my 356, August 8, 1 p.m.,Not printed. Eden and Vansittart arrived in Paris on TuesdayAugust 13. evening to conduct preliminary conversations before the arrival of Aloisi. Foreign Office received last evening a report of the Wednesday conversations in which Vansittart indicated that the French still harbored two illusions: (1) that the issue lay mainly between the opposing policies of England and Italy, and (2) that Laval’s role was to act as mediator between these points of view which implied that there could be imposed arbitrarily upon Abyssinia a settlement to which he, as mediator, could induce the British and Italian Governments to agree. Vansittart added that the Wednesday discussions had been mainly taken up in disabusing the French of these impressions and pointing out the urgency for a common Anglo-French front in settlement of an Italian-Abyssinian question. Foreign Office has received no report of yesterday’s conversations and they have asked me to see them tomorrow morning.
